name: test_user_logout
description: Automated Test Case: Verify that a logged-in user can successfully log out of the application.
descriptionHtml: Automated Test Case: Verify that a logged-in user can successfully log out of the application.
statusTrace:
page = <Page url='https://tutorialsninja.com/demo/index.php?route=account/logout'>

    @pytest.mark.sanity
    def test_user_logout(page):
        """
            Automated Test Case: Verify that a logged-in user can successfully log out of the application.
            """
    
        # --- Step 1: Create Page Object Instances ---
        home_page = HomePage(page)
        login_page = LoginPage(page)
        my_account_page = MyAccountPage(page)
    
        # --- Step 2: Navigate to Login Page ---
        home_page.click_my_account()
        home_page.click_login()
    
        # --- Step 3: Enter Valid Credentials and Login ---
        login_page.set_email(Config.email)
        login_page.set_password(Config.password)
        login_page.click_login()
        time.sleep(5)
    
        # --- Step 4: Verify 'My Account' Page is Displayed ---
        user_account_displayed = my_account_page.get_my_account_page_heading()
        expect(user_account_displayed).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
    
        # --- Step 5: Perform Logout Action ---
        my_account_page.click_logout()
    
        # --- Step 6: Check Logout text is visible or not ---
        logout_text = my_account_page.get_logout_text_visible_()
        expect(logout_text).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        # --- Step 7: Click 'Continue' to Return to Home Page ---
>       my_account_page.click_continue()
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
E       AttributeError: 'MyAccountPage' object has no attribute 'click_continue'

tests\test_user_logout.py:66: AttributeError
